data.body:
Plan for epic 850f (Code-grounding T2). Seam recon done: T2 is the tier vocab (TIER_T2, defined, never emitted) + the inline member/dotted abstain branch in resolve.refute_absence (resolve.py:443, tagged T1 today). Facade oracle.refute_absence is the single consumer entry; oracle._backend_availability() builds grounding_info. Contract via evidence.py builders (refuted/match/abstain), all consumers pass through normalize_evidence so valid T2 records flow unchanged. Config = .rebar/grounding.toml via GroundingConfig/load_config. Fail-open harness = harness.run_tool.

Decomposition (3 stacked children, bottom->top):
S1 ADR: select v1 backend = one-shot compiler-CLI diagnostics (pyright/Python); confirm-only mapping (clean run + no diagnostic at ref + imports resolved -> refuted@T2; any diagnostic or tool failure -> abstain; NEVER assert absent); defer LSP-lib + containerized; split IaC lane into a sibling epic.
S2 Seam: pluggable T2Backend protocol + registry + config keys (t2_enabled/t2_backend) + grounding_info mechanism; escalate member/dotted + 0-def symbol from resolve/oracle to the T2 backend; default-off => regression-safe, consumers unchanged.
S3 Backend: pyright --outputjson diagnostics backend behind S2 seam; optional extra grounding-t2; self-registers into grounding_info; docs/grounding.md T2 section; per-backend fail-open matrix.

Each child = one Gerrit change in a stack; land all once the epic completes. Gates are live (plan-review before claim; completion-verifier before close).
